title: Mercimek Çorbası (Turkish Red Lentil Soup)

overview.description: A smooth, comforting Turkish soup made from red lentils, vegetables and homemade chicken stock, finished with a fragrant butter, paprika and mint drizzle.

ingredients: 1 tsp sweet paprika or pul biber (Aleppo pepper)
method: Melt the butter over a medium heat.
Cook the onion and carrot for 8–10 minutes until softened.
Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin and cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in the chicken stock and water.
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer.
Cook for 25–30 minutes until the lentils are completely soft.
Season with salt and black pepper.
For the topping, melt the remaining butter.
Stir in the paprika (or pul biber) and dried mint.
Serve the soup with the butter drizzled over the top and lemon wedges alongside.

learning.did-you-know: Mercimek Çorbası is one of Turkey's most popular everyday soups and is commonly served before almost any main meal.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to make a smooth lentil soup and finish it with an infused butter that adds aroma and richness.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick, inexpensive soup built almost entirely from pantry staples that freezes beautifully and delivers a lot of flavour for very little effort.
chapman-tips: Rinse the lentils well before cooking to remove excess starch and dust.
Blend thoroughly for the characteristic silky texture.
The paprika and mint butter is what makes this soup distinctly Turkish—don't skip it.
Always serve with fresh lemon wedges so everyone can adjust the acidity to their taste.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze in family-sized portions for up to 6 months. Prepare the butter topping fresh when serving.
notes.leftovers: The soup thickens as it stands; loosen with a little stock or water when reheating.
